title: Alaska 2.0
summary: Federal funding has long been crucial to Alaska’s economy, even before statehood. title: Alaska’s Indigenous Peoples Day
summary: Alaska is one of only a handful of states that celebrates Indigenous Peoples Day on the federal Columbus Day holiday. The state holiday was first created by annual proclamations in 2015 and 2016 by then Governor Bill Walker. In 2017, the Alaska legislature made the holiday permanent, and Walker sign...

summary: Above: The Ascension Church of Our Lord Chapel at Karluk on Kodiak Island. Photo by Tommy’s Dog Climate change is impacting cultural sites across Alaska. title: New Yuletide Festival Headlines Fair’s Holiday Season
summary: There are more ways than ever to celebrate the holidays at the fairgrounds this year.
